title: Atika Syeda
text: I am a PhD student in neuroscience in the joint Johns Hopkins University–HHMI Janelia Research Campus Graduate Program, where I am jointly advised by the Stringer & Pachitariu Lab. My research lies at the intersection of neuroscience and artificial intelligence, with a particular focus on developing machine learning methods to better understand large-scale neural datasets.

title: Research
text:
Research Interests
My research lies at the intersection of neuroscience and artificial intelligence, with a focus on leveraging machine learning to understand complex neural systems. I am particularly interested in developing machine learning tools for studying large-scale neuroscience datasets.

Neuroscience
Artificial Intelligence
Machine Learning
Deep Learning
Computational Neuroscience
Large-scale Data Analysis




Projects

1. Facemap
I developed Facemap for predicting activity of ~50,000 neurons recorded with 2-photon calcium imaging in mice. Facemap predicts neural activity driven by mouse orofacial movements across cortical regions. It includes pose estimation for tracking facial keypoints and a neural network model that maps behavior to neural responses.
Tools: Python, TensorFlow, MATLAB
Collaborators: Add collaborators or lab names here
Link: Facemap





Research Tools
I employ a variety of methodologies in my research, including:

Data Analysis: Statistical modeling, machine learning, and signal processing for neural data
Programming Languages: Python, MATLAB, R, Java
Machine Learning Frameworks: PyTorch, TensorFlow

title: Software
text:
Facemap
            I developed Facemap for predicting activity of ~50,000 neurons recorded with 2-photon calcium imaging in mice. Facemap predicted activity driven by mouse orofacial movements across different cortical regions. It includes a pose estimation model for tracking distinct keypoints on the mouse face and a neural network model for predicting neural activity using the pose estimates.

            Rastermap
            I also worked on developing GUI for rastermap algorithm. Rastermap is a discovery algorithm for neural data. The algorithm was written by Carsen Stringer and Marius Pachitariu.

            Tongue segmentation
            This project focuses on image segmentation of soft-body objects using various convolution neural networks. Specifically, the project uses data from a high-speed imaging camera to perform image segmentation to identify and track a soft-body object, namely the tongue, from static images of mouse faces recorded from a bottom view camera.
